name: Fungal Ringworm (Tinea Capitis)
description: A highly contagious superficial fungal infection of the scalp and hair shafts. Thrives in warm, humid conditions. Triggers circular scaly patches, hair breakage, alopecia, and severe itching.

climateLinks.impact: Sweating and skin dampness create a fertile environment for fungal spores to colonize the stratum corneum and hair follicles.

prevention: Washing hair and skin regularly with soap
Avoiding sharing combs, towels, hats, or bedding
Keeping school classrooms and children's dormitories dry and well-ventilated
triageAdvisory: Confirm through Wood's lamp fluorescence or skin scraping microscopy. Treat superficial patches with topical antifungals. Extensive scalp ringworm (tinea capitis) requires oral Griseofulvin or Terbinafine.
